commit_scope: Release /api/utils/kernel_status from Python authority by serving it from the native kernel-router manifest.

local_validation.summary: The production kernel-router manifest now includes a Form-native /api/utils/kernel_status handler. The handler reports active=native-kernel, router=native-kernel, python_authority=false, and live router counters from the same request context that feeds /api/attention/kernel-runtime.

observed_delta.promotion_safety: Existing promoted compute-route parity remains green: 109 route/query checks value-identical to the local CPython oracle; /api/health still fans out with X-Form-Router=fanout-python.

known_limitations.observed: api/app/routers/utils.py still exposes the bridge status endpoint for dev/fanout contexts. This slice removes Python authority when the production kernel-router manifest serves the route; it does not delete the compatibility row yet.

deploy_validation.notes: Post-merge deploy validation should confirm X-Form-Router:native-kernel and python_authority:false publicly.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: GET /api/utils/kernel_status is now kernel-first capable in the production route manifest and can be served without asking FastAPI/Python whether the kernel is available.
test_flows: Boot the real FastAPI app as oracle and the kernel-router with deploy/kernel-router/production-routes.fk.

Call /api/utils/kernel_status through the kernel-router and require HTTP 200, X-Form-Router:native-kernel, Content-Type:application/json, active:native-kernel, router:native-kernel, python_authority:false.
Confirm /api/health still fans out so the promoted route does not erase explicit Python fallback for unpromoted paths.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete. CI, merge, deploy, and public observation remain before claiming public release.
